---
title: "Latin Square: Shortcut Comparison"
author: "Axel Baudot"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    theme: cosmo
---

```{python}
import experiment_api as api
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Set log level to INFO
api.set_log_level('INFO')

branchings = ["max_marginal", "max_marginal_regret", "max_marginal_strength", "min_entropy", "min_normalized_entropy"]
group_by = ['propagation_shortcut']
```

# Sum-Product Belief Propagation

In this section, we compare different thresholds to branch on entropy with Sum-Product BP

```{python}
# SQL condition to select runs with Sum-Product BP
for branching in branchings:
    print(branching)
    sql_conditions = f"""
        (search_type = 'lds'
        AND
        bp = 'sum_product'
        AND
        branching = '{branching}'
        AND
        (oracle = 1.0 AND max_iter = 10 and entropy_branching_threshold < 1.0))
    """

    # Create comprehensive comparison plot
    fig1 = api.plot_comprehensive_comparison(
        sql_conditions=sql_conditions,
        group_by=group_by,
        title="Comparison of Branching Strategies (LDS)"
    )

    plt.show()
```

# Max-Product Belief Propagation

In this section, we compare different thresholds to branch on entropy using Max-Product BP

```{python}
for branching in branchings:
    print(branching)
    sql_conditions = f"""
        (search_type = 'lds'
        AND
        bp = 'max_product'
        AND
        branching = '{branching}'
        AND
        (oracle = 1.0 AND max_iter = 10 and entropy_branching_threshold = 1.0))
    """

    # Create comprehensive comparison plot
    fig1 = api.plot_comprehensive_comparison(
        sql_conditions=sql_conditions,
        group_by=group_by,
        title="Comparison of Branching Strategies (LDS)"
    )

    plt.show()
```
